conversations.say: Inez, that boundary is exactly right: no uninvolved names, no route details, and no introductions without mutual consent and a concrete protection. I forgave the balance publicly; now I need a neighbor to say that choice protects the neighborhood rather than hides an identity.
conversations.offer: I will enforce the same boundary for your contacts and refuse any ledger access that exposes local customers or side arrangements.
conversations.request: Publicly support the forgiveness as a neighborhood-protective choice, while stating that the account and ledger do not identify R.

conversations.say: Morgan, I accept the protected route-comparison terms. O06 can date a disputed transaction-pattern change, but it contains no firsthand sighting by me that identifies Vale on your route, and it cannot identify an R sender.
conversations.offer: I will show O06 again and explain only the dated pattern relevant to your comparison.
conversations.request: Credit my records, omit customers and side arrangements, and bring any apparent conflict to a protected conference with Inez and me before reporting it.

conversations.say: Dee, I accept the firsthand-only rule. Your camera gap establishes an unidentified use of the service exit; my ledger pattern cannot supply the user's identity.
conversations.offer: I will compare only timing and transaction context that does not expose a customer or side arrangement.
conversations.request: Tell me whether your access routine independently dates the disputed change, and keep all uninvolved names and route details behind the boundary I share with Inez.

public_statement: I forgave the outstanding balance deliberately. I am asking Inez to support that choice as neighborhood protection, not as proof about R; the ledger remains a record of incompatible customers and a disputed pattern change, not a single-person identity card.

commitments.promise: Neither of us will identify uninvolved neighbors or disclose route details without mutual consent and a concrete protection.
Mutually protect local customer identities and side arrangements.
Use the account only as a bounded route comparison and invite a protected conference before drawing a conclusion from any conflict.
We will compare only firsthand service-route observations and will not agree on an identity neither of us saw.
Publicly support Rafi’s forgiveness of the outstanding account without treating the ledger as proof of R’s identity.

correction: The recurring habit and disputed change date do not establish one customer, identify an R sender, or identify the person who used the service exit.
goal_progress: Maintained the deliberate public forgiveness of the outstanding account.
Accepted Inez’s mutual disclosure boundary and requested concrete neighborhood support.
Traded Morgan bounded ledger access for attribution, privacy, and protected-conflict terms.
Accepted Dee’s firsthand-only route comparison without supplying an identity theory.
reasoning_summary: Rafi protects the neighborhood story by securing reciprocal privacy terms, limiting Morgan and Dee to bounded comparisons, and seeking Inez’s public support for forgiveness. The ledger’s incompatible customers make a progressive or mantle interpretation more plausible than a single continuous person, while proving no identity or fate.
